dialogos.texto_en: The botanical garden within the Native Okinawan Village\nhad many rare plants, right?
They have a different concept compared to other \nbotanical gardens.\nThey seem to be collecting native species from Okinawa.
So, there were cherry blossom trees too,\nbut are they also native to Okinawa?
Yeah, 'hikanzakura,'\nalso known as 'kanhizakura.'
I read the description, and it's not a native species,\nbut apparently it's a type of cherry blossom\nthat is commonly found in Okinawa.
The trees have already finished blooming, haven't they?
In Okinawa, they start blooming from late January,\nso the best time to see them has long passed.
It's a shame we couldn't see it,\nbut there's a photo of it in the brochure. Look.
Oh, this is what the flower looks like.\nIt's so pretty with its deep pink color!
It's quite different from the somei-yoshino.
And it blooms quite early, doesn't it?
As expected from the tropics.\nThey're already blooming on Honshu.
It seems like it's gonna be a little difficult\nto see cherry blossoms with you, Futaro, isn't it?\nI wanted to have a cherry blossom viewing date.
Yeah...\nThere are things we need to do for our new life,\nand we're both busy, aren't we?
It's impossible this year.\nIt's obvious that I'll be busy with the move.
That's right.
Sorry...
You don't have to apologize, Futaro.\nSpring's the season for that kind of thing.
But, let's go see them together next year.\nIt's a promise.
Yeah, of course.
If we're going to view cherry blossoms nearby,\nI definitely want to bring bento with us.
Will you make one for me?
We might be able to see a little bit of it.\nI'll be busy with the move, but we'll have time\nto take a walk around the neighborhood.
Really? That's fine,\nlet's go after we get back from Okinawa!
I really think we'll only have time for a walk,\nbut is that okay?
What's important is to make memories with you, Futaro.\nHow long we do something doesn't matter.
Of course, to be honest,\nI'd like to spend some quality time with you.
But even if it's not possible this year,\nwe'll have next year, right, Futaro?
Okay, got it.
If we're going to view cherry blossoms nearby,\nI definitely want to bring bento with us.
Will you make one for me?
Let's go see cherry blossoms next year.\nIt would be nice to even go to a so-called famous spot.
You mean like, on a trip?
Well... I don't mind if it was on a trip, you know?
Yay!\nAlright, let's go, you and me!
Teehee, I can think of so many things I want to do now.\nI'm really excited!
Sorry for this year,\nbut let's enjoy ourselves next year.
Is this year really impossible?
Sorry, I have to move and all that.\nSo, I don't think I'll have time.
But, well, I might be able to take a walk\naround the neighborhood.
In that case,\nlet's go for a cherry blossom viewing walk.\nEven if it's just for a little bit, okay?
If we're going to view cherry blossoms nearby,\nI definitely want to bring bento with us.
Will you make one for me?
Of course! What kind of bento would you like to eat,\nFutaro?
Anything's fine%p-2;――
Don't say anything's fine.\nTell me what you really want to eat.
Alright, then....
Rice balls or something?
Why are you asking in a questioning tone?\nFutaro, you always make things difficult\nin situations like this.
But, rice balls it is.\nGot any specific filling requests?
I'll leave it up to you...\nor, maybe no fillings?
Hmm, this time I'll stuff the rice balls for you.
But, I might make rice balls that\nyou've never eaten before, Futaro.\nYou won't complain, will you?
Wh-what kind of rice balls are you talking about?
Teehee, you'd best be excited!
